body: OpenCode's `opencode run --session <id>` chaining IS session resume — each invocation after the first joins the existing SQLite session row. The daemon observes the same ses_… id across PID lifetimes (process exits between turns, then a fresh process re-emits transcript activity for the same session). Recording validates 4/4 phases: session_birth, turn1 cycle, session_resumed (same id re-appears).
caveats: Session continuity is via SQLite, not a persistent PID. Irrlicht's PID-liveness check doesn't apply across invocations; continuity is implicit in the session table.
Coverage matrix notes ParentSessionID isn't populated recursively for resume chains — that's a separate gap from this scenario's narrower 'same session id reappears' assertion.
